title: Showing revisions as their own cards
question: How can you see every revision a decision went through without opening the decision and scrolling to its history?
rationale: In the Recent view, each revision shows as its own slim card at the moment it happened, alongside the decision's creation card — so the timeline shows every change as a distinct event. The revision card links back to its decision and opens straight to the history. Chosen because it answers 'what changed and when' at a glance without duplicating data: the decision file's history list stays the single source of truth. Within this stream, cards in the 'Last 24 hours' bucket show the time instead of the date, since the day is already given.

history.from: Recent cards showed the date for every event, including those in the 'Last 24 hours' bucket
history.reason: Cards and rows in the 'Last 24 hours' bucket now show the time (e.g. 13:12) instead of the date — the day is already given by the bucket, so the time is the useful detail; the other buckets keep showing the date, and anything with no stamped time falls back to the date

history.from: The revision card and the revision list row didn't match — the card showed an extra 'was …' line and tinted its '↻ Revision' label with the phase colour
history.reason: Made the two views consistent: both now show the same revision text (the reason, falling back to 'was …'), and the '↻ Revision' label is gold on both, matching the 'revised' badge — the full before/after still lives in the timeline

options.label: Each revision is its own card in Recent, linked to the decision

tradeoffs.note: every change is a visible event in the timeline, not buried at the bottom of a decision
revision cards are built from the existing history list at view time — no change to how decisions are stored
scoped to the Recent stream; the By phase / version / area groupings keep one card per decision with a 'revised' badge, so they don't double up

options.label: Promote each revision to a full decision record

tradeoffs.note: duplicates data and breaks the 'a revision edits the one decision file' flow; two places to keep in sync

options.label: Keep revisions only inside the decision's detail (status quo)

tradeoffs.note: you must open a decision and scroll to the bottom to see what changed — the change isn't visible in the timeline
